entry.title: Re: Can AS2 Sender iFlow be exposed with Basic Authentication via API Management?
entry.content:

Thank you for the response.

I was planning to implement this API Management (APIM) solution due to a recurring issue where certain partners are encountering a 401 Unauthorized error when attempting to connect to the AS2 sender at the IFlow level. I have created a specific client ID and client secret for these partners and shared them accordingly. Additionally, I have attempted to use client certificate-based authentication; however, none of these solutions have resolved the issue, as they continue to result in a 401 Unauthorized error. Could you please suggest any alternative approaches or workarounds to address this specific problem if APIM can't be utilized here?

Thanks,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: SAP API Management - API Response Message Size and Page Size
entry.content:
Hi @Elijah_Martinez1,

I am facing a challenge, and it's also related to response message size and link of pagination in the response body. When I enable streaming to true due to the large response payload from S/4, the API response paginates because there are too many records. The "next" URL points to S/4 instead of APIM. with streaming enabled, the API policy can't modify the URL from S/4 to APIM. This is causing confusion for the API consumer program. Do you have any suggestions? Thanks so much!

Regards,

Nick

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Mass Maintenance of Sales Are...
entry.content:
Hello @LukasKuhn 

Thank you for your question.

In Mass Maintenance, the system only permits you to change specific table. For KNVV_STXL, inserting action is not supported. So, you can only change existing rows but not to create a new Sales Area record by importing a file. This is why your upload got rejected. Below are answers to your questions.

Question: is there another action code that can achieve this? If not, what is another way to add Sales Texts to business partners in mass?

Answer: No. Since the table does not permit you to insert in the app, changing the action code will not help.

  • You can add Sales Area Texts in mass by maintaining the BP UI if volume is small
  • Alternatively, you can use the released Customer Sales Text API via the Business Partner OData API. This allows you to create text entries in public cloud, instead of trying to insert via Mass Maintenance. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/85043858ea0f9244e10000000a4450e5.html for more details.

Here is a quick guide:

Open Communications Arrangements,

Create a new arrangement for SAP_COM_0008,

Create or assign a Communication User and generate credentials.

Make sure you add your Hostname and save. Call the OData service API_BUSINESS_PARTNER and update the Customer Sales Area Text for the designated customer and sales area.

Question: My question is, is this table and field out of scope for the MDG processes? If so, is there an alternative way to maintain it in mass?

Answer: Yes, this is restricted in public cloud. So, ADRT is not part of the mass import/export scope in public cloud.

The public cloud provides the following options for Email remarks/notes:

  • You can use the BP communication data UI, if the note is for email. However, not ideal for mass creations.
  • You can maintain communication remarks through the Business Partner API.  This is the most scalable public cloud option. This is described in KBA 3403243 and Business Partner (A2X) – OData API.

To summarise:

  • Mass Maintenance cannot insert into KNVV_STXL. There is no action code workaround.
  • Use API_BUSINESS_PARTNER for mass creation of sales text and not MDG-style inserts.
  • Email notes (ADRT) not being exportable in Mass Maintenance is defined in public cloud’s scope.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Staging for Kanban Using Warehouse Tasks: Negative st...
entry.content:
Hi @Niklas_Anton ,
This is a known limitation of Kanban replenishment with control type 8 (Stock Transfer with Warehouse Task) in embedded EWM when nested HUs are involved. The system posts goods issue per sub-HU instead of at the main HU level, which can lead to negative stock. SAP has acknowledged this behavior in community discussions, and there are no standard corrections yet—only design recommendations

This is a limitation of embedded EWM Kanban staging with control type 8. No SAP Note fixes it yet; recommended workarounds are avoiding nested HUs, using delivery-based replenishment, or implementing a custom enhancement.

entry.title: Re: F1705 - Inbound delivery EWM cannot be created on ERP...
entry.content:
Hi @mauriciocastiblanco ,
As per your observation, and the behavior you’re describing is actually consistent with how EWM–ERP integration can be configured. It doesn’t necessarily point to a queue issue, but rather to the delivery replication trigger point in your setup.

This is not a queue problem but a process configuration issue. Your system is currently set up so that the ERP delivery is only created after unloading/packing in EWM. If you want the ERP delivery to appear immediately after inbound delivery creation in EWM, you’ll need to adjust the delivery replication process profile/customizing so that replication occurs at creation rather than after warehouse activities.

entry.title: Re: Impact of Time-Dependent Service Cost Level on Progre...
entry.content:
Hi Minsung_Lee, 

In the Review Customer Projects app, the Percentage of Completion (PoC) is calculated based on the total planned cost (sum across all periods) of the ongoing plan version of the project.
It does not use the baseline version for PoC calculations.

This means that when time‑dependent service cost levels vary across periods, the system relies on the planned costs from the currently active (ongoing) plan version, not the original baseline. As a result, the planned‑cost denominator used for PoC reflects the updated period‑specific rates included in the ongoing plan.

Hope this helps in understanding.

Kind Regards

Harish Gour

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Accruals – Configuration in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi together,

has anybody experience or knows how to configure or influence the posting date of the period posting run for e.g. the PO accruals/deferrals ?
By default the posting date is always the last day of the current period, but our business would like to have e.g. the deferrals posted already on the 1st day of the period.

The reason for this is that the costs that were already posted with the prepaid invoice can then already be checked even at the start of the month. In case of missing amounts and booking errors, they will have a full month to check and request adjustments.
Currently, the business colleagues must wait till the end of the month to make sure all postings are done as requested which leaves very little time to make changes in case of errors during the monthly closing process.

Best Regards,
Günter HUMPF

entry.title: Re: In cycle counting, how do we add found material?
entry.content: Best practice in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is to formally record any “found” material through the physical inventory process, not just write it in. You should create a handling unit or assign the material to a storage bin, then post the inventory adjustment so finance sees a controlled, auditable entry. This avoids uncontrolled postings that frustrate finance and ensures compliance with audit requirements.

entry.title: Re: Fetch data in Adobe forms using standard cds in SAP P...
entry.content:
Good day @Rakshna 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, there are alternatives. However, it also comes with some limitations.

Does your data exist in standard released form data sources? If the answer to that question is yes, then use Data Source Extension with standard fields. Bind those fields in the custom form template.

See the  following references for more details https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/579920be820f45969e061197aca34484.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/fc1eb1eddef8476b8a6438256e73e7bc.html

If the answer is no, then the public cloud only supports adding custom fields that is filled via a released BAdI/custom logic and then expose in form data source. This is just your normal standard extensibility pattern forms offers. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/65f5fd6ac102431fab0974c19c24f220.html for more insights.

Here is a quick check guide:

In your Maintain Form Templates app, check whether the fields you require are already available.

If you don’t find it, then create a Data Source Extension in the Custom Logic app and include available standard fields first. Save the creation and publish. Open the form template extension and bind the new fields you exposed.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Is it possible in Public Cloud to generate the Qualit...
entry.content: Yes – in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud you can automatically trigger a quality notification when a defect is recorded during inspection lot results, but it requires proper configuration of defect recording and notification assignment. The system does not create notifications by default; you must set up inspection characteristics and notification types to link defects with notifications. Check SAP note 3599756

entry.title: Re: Sequence in which QA10 transaction (RQEVAI30) picks t...
entry.content:
Selection Logic: QA10 does not inherently process inspection lots in the order of creation. Instead, it processes lots based on:

  • The selection criteria you define in the variant (material, plant, inspection type, date ranges, etc.).
  • The internal sort order of the program, which typically uses inspection lot numbers but not strictly in ascending creation sequence.

Batch Job Behavior: When run in background, QA10 pulls all eligible lots and processes them. If multiple lots exist for the same material/batch, the program doesn’t guarantee chronological order — it may pick the latest lot first, then older ones, depending on how the database query returns results.

The safest approach is either:

  • Configure the variant to only pick the correct/latest lot, or
  • Handle duplicate batch lots manually to avoid conflicts.

entry.title: Re: Error while saving sales invoices - In comp.cd , the...
entry.content:
This is a tricky one, but the error message F5 153 “In comp.cd , the number range for 2026 has external numbers” is actually pointing to the accounting document number range setup, not the billing document number range.

To your question "Why it only affects some users, this is the confusing part. The difference usually comes from:

Transaction Variant / Customizing Differences
Some users may be using a transaction variant or a different billing type that points to a different number range object.

User Parameters (SU3)
Check if any user parameter (like XINNR) is set differently for those users, forcing external number entry.

Authorization Groups
Even if there’s no outright authorization error, some users may be routed to a different number range interval due to customizing in document type → number range assignment.

Fiscal Year Dependency
Number ranges are year-dependent. If 2026 is not properly maintained for all intervals, some users may hit the wrong one.

The error is not about authorizations but about number range assignment mismatch. It’s not really user-specific — it’s that some users trigger a different posting path (different billing type, different document type assignment). Check FBN1 for 2026, confirm the accounting document type used by billing is linked to an internal number range, and ensure no external range is accidentally assigned.

entry.title: Github Copilot VSCode - My Own SAP Development Rules
entry.content:
Lightweight Governance for SAP CAP and UI5 Projects

Creating clear, consistent project rules is one of the simplest ways to improve collaboration and quality in SAP CAP and UI5 projects. In this workspace, the guidance is organized into focused files: backend rules for CAP, frontend rules for UI5, Jest testing rules, and Bruno collection rules. Each file sets a crisp scope and avoids ambiguity, which is essential when multiple developers or tools contribute changes.

Why this structure works

- **CAP rules** enforce reusable types, managed data, soft-delete behavior, and strict service conventions.
- **UI5 rules** define naming standards, view constraints, and annotation boundaries, keeping frontend development aligned with backend services.
- **Testing rules** establish baseline quality by requiring meaningful Jest coverage for custom logic.
- **Bruno rules** standardize API collection formats, which keeps integration testing reliable and portable.

Together, these files form a lightweight governance layer. They improve onboarding, reduce review cycles, and keep the codebase predictable. For teams working across SAP CAP and SAP UI5, this approach offers a practical template for consistency without sacrificing speed.

Call to action

If your team ships SAP CAP and UI5 solutions, consider creating a similar ruleset and share your approach with the SAP Community. Small, well-scoped guidelines can make a big difference in day-to-day delivery.

Download, Use and Improve the rules:
https://github.com/radu103/github-copilot-vscode-sap-dev-instructions/

entry.title: India Process Ewaybill for PO Return Delivery Question about creating Return outside of SAP
entry.content:
Scenario Summary (India GST) for Purchase Order Return

Buyer and vendor are in the same state — intra-state movement
PO IncoTerms are EXW — buyer picks up good from vendor with their own truck. Thus, eway bill is responsibility of buyer.
PO: 10 pcs × ₹30,000 each → goods picked up by buyer
Buyer finds 3 damaged items and plans to return them, and vendor replaces. Being EXW me as a buyer goes and return and also gets the replacement. 
Buyer does not want to create a formal SAP return delivery, but we need e-way bill in order to return goods to vendor.

Question:

➡ Can we create the return delivery for the 3 damaged items outside of SAP and use the return challan created outside SAP to manually generate the e-Way Bill? Would this be GST compliant?

➡ When we collect the 3 replacement items, we understand that a separate e-Way Bill is required. Could you please advise what reference document should be used to generate the e-Way Bill for these replacement items so that we can transport the goods back to our plant?

Below would be the process flow
Part 1 — Initial Pickup under EXW

Since terms are EXW:

✔ You (buyer) cause movement ✔ Goods move from vendor premises to your site ✔ Goods value = 10 × 30,000 = ₹300,000 > ₹50,000 threshold, so you me as buyer would generate an e-Way Bill on the GST e-Way Bill portal before pickup.

Part 2 — Return of (3 damaged items)

You found 3 damaged items and want to return them to the vendor.
We do not want to create the return document in SAP. Can we create it outside of SAP. Create the return delivery challan outside SAP.

Part 3 — Replacement (3 damaged items)

The vendor provides replacement of the 3 damaged items still outside SAP. Not sure about the reference doc to use to get eway bill for me as a buyer to transport it back to my premise.

PS. If maybe i am misunderstanding something about ewaybill please correct me.

entry.title: How to Deliver Customizing as a SaaS Provider using Business Configuration Sets
entry.content:
In our previous blog post, How to Provide Customizing as a SaaS Provider, we described a programmatic approach that used EML statements with explicitly coded object templates in application jobs to deliver customizing entries. With release 2511, you can now use Business Configuration Sets instead, removing the need to include explicitly coded customizing data in your business logic.

This blog illustrates how to maintain customizing data in a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, how to maintain customizing translations, importing the created customizing data/translations into a BC Set, implementing an application job for the BC Set activation and finally how to deploy the BC Set content to the relevant customizing table.
We will also have a look into how BC Sets can be updated programmatically for the case when the customizing data was changed and BC Set data needs to be reimported.

This blog presents an alternative method to the Fiori app Upload Business Configuration. One limitation of the Upload Business Configuration app is that it does not validate the customizing data being uploaded.
In contrast, customizing data imported into Business Configuration (BC) Sets via ADT originates from existing customizing requests. This data is created within custom business configuration objects, where it is automatically validated through the underlying BO behavior (for example, error codes greater than 500 are rejected):

This blog covers the following topics:

  • Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object
  • Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object
  • Creating customizing data & translations
  • Creating a BC Set and importing customizing data & translations
  • Creating an Application Job for the BC Set activation
  • Deploying the BC Set content by running the application job
  • Maintaining customizing data programmatically in BC Sets
  • Summary

Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object

The Business Configuration Maintenance Object discussed in this blog can be developed using the following tutorial, which guides you in creating a table for storing error codes:

Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials

Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object

To use the Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the required authorization must be set up. How to do this is explained in this tutorial:

Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials

Creating customizing data & translations

Maintain following customizing data as described in tutorial Use Custom Business Configurations app on a new customizing request:

Error Code

Description

400

Bad Request

401

Unauthorized

403

Forbidden

Afterwards your customizing request should include the primary customizing data as Customizing: Table Contents. Such customizing requests can be used for importing into a BC Set. Corresponding data records and translations will be extracted automatically during the process.

Maintain customizing translations for source language English United States and target language, for example German Germany, for the error codes using the Maintain Customizing Translations app.

Select the text table ZERRCODET_### that was created earlier as text source in your translation project.

Creating a BC Set and importing customizing data & translations

Follow the steps provided in Creating Business Configuration Sets to create a new business configuration set and use the customizing request that was created in the previous step for importing the data.

After successful import from the customizing request the error code data records as well as translations are included:

We can now proceed to implement an application job that can be used activate this BC Set.

Note: To import customizing data into a BC Set, the customizing data needs to be maintained for customizing objects of type Individual Transaction Object. A business configuration maintenance object (CUBCO) is configured as such by defining a transport object definition (TOBJ) in the service configuration settings of the CUBCO. By using the business configuration maintenance object wizard as described in Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials, this prerequisite is fulfilled. For more information, please refer to Generating a Business Configuration Maintenance Object with the Generate ABAP Repository Objects Wizard → BC Management. Configuration changes are then recorded with object type TDAT instead of a TABU.

Those business configuration maintenance objects without existing transport object definition must be migrated, and a transport object definition needs to be created manually. How this can be done is described in the blog post How to enable BC Set support for BC app.

Creating an Application Job for the BC Set activation

In this example, an Application Job is used for creating and updating the customizing entries in a SaaS tenant. The customizing entries are deployed within the Application Job's implementation by activating the business configuration set using the corresponding runtime API.

As a first step, implement the BC Set activation as the business logic of the of the application job with a parameter for the BC Set to be activated and while saving activation messages in the application log:

CLASS zcl_errorcode_set_activate_000 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized">Error Codes BC Set</p>
    DATA errorcode_bc_set TYPE cl_scpr_cd_bcset_activation=>ty_bcset_id VALUE 'Z_ERROR_CODES_SET_000'.

    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run.
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_defaults.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CONSTANTS log_object    TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_object    VALUE 'Z_ERRORCODE_SET_000'.
    CONSTANTS log_subobject TYPE cl_bali_header_setter=>ty_subobject VALUE 'ACTIVATE'.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_errorcode_set_activate_000 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_apj_dt_defaults~fill_attribute_defaults.
    errorcode_bc_set = 'Z_ERROR_CODES_SET_000'.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_apj_rt_run~execute.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_bali_log) = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
                                header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object    = log_object
                                                                        subobject = log_subobject ) ).
        TRY.
            DATA(lo_bcset_activate) = cl_scpr_cd_bcset_activation=>of( errorcode_bc_set ).
          CATCH cx_scpr_object_not_permitted INTO DATA(lx_scpr_object_not_permitted).
            lo_bali_log->add_item(
                item = cl_bali_exception_setter=>create( severity  = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                         exception = lx_scpr_object_not_permitted ) ).
        ENDTRY.

        DATA(lo_activation_handler) = lo_bcset_activate->get_activate_handler( ).

        DATA(lo_activation_result) = lo_activation_handler->local( )->add_activation_options( )->start( ).

        DATA(lt_activation_messages) = lo_activation_result->get_messages( ).

        LOOP AT lt_activation_messages INTO DATA(ls_msg).
          lo_bali_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_bapiret2( message_data = ls_msg ) ).
        ENDLOOP.

        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log_2nd_db_connection( log                        = lo_bali_log
                                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

      CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(lx_bali_runtime).
        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_apj_rt_content USING MESSAGE
          EXPORTING previous = lx_bali_runtime.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Then create a catalog entry and job template for the class with the business logic: Creating a Job Catalog Entry and a Job Template. Also create an application log object and sub object according to the job logic implementation: Working with Application Log Objects.

To run the application job for BC Set activation, users must have the authorization provided by a business catalog. In this case we will reuse the business catalog Z_ERROR_CODES_### that was already created as part of the tutorial Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object.

The business catalog Z_ERROR_CODES_### already includes the IAM App of type MBC (Business Configuration App) that was created for the CUBCO. During creation of the job catalog entry for the BC Set activation job, another IAM app with name <job catalog entry name>_SAJC is automatically created (refer to Setting up the Authorizations). This IAM app, to start the application job, is added to the existing business catalog so that the catalog provides authorizations to maintain error codes in the CUBCO as well as to start the BC Set activation job:

The BC Set activation implemented in the activation job using the CL_SCPR_CD_BCSET_ACTIVATION API requires table access for all entities used in the BC Set, so for both the primary tables as well as text tables. This can be achieved by adding the names of the tables in the TABLE authorization field of the existing S_TABU_NAM authorization in the _MBC IAM app: in this case ZERRCODET_000 (text table) and ZERRCODE_000 (primary table):

Hint: If you plan to run the BC Set activation job in your development system, do not forget to publish your changes to the business catalog locally.

Deploying the BC Set content by running the application job

Make sure that the user that shall run the BC Set activation job has following business roles/catalogs assigned in the ABAP tenant (for example your SaaS consumer tenant):

Business Role

Business Catalog

Purpose

SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

SAP_CORE_BC_APJ_JCE

For scheduling jobs in the Application Jobs app

SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_MBC_PC

To use the Custom Business Configurations app

ZBR_ERROR_CODES_EXPERT_###

Z_ERROR_CODES_###

To maintain error codes in the Custom Business Configurations app and to start the BC Set activation job.
As created in tutorial Providing Authorization Control for a Business Configuration Maintenance Object

Open the Application Job App and select "Create" to create a new Application Job, then
choose the Application Job Template. Maintain the scheduling options and provide a valid BC Set in the parameter. Schedule the Application Job by clicking "Schedule".

Check the Application Job Log to determine if the BC Set content was deployed correctly:

Users assigned to the required business roles (see above) can now check in the Custom Business Configurations App whether the customizing has been inserted and updated correctly:

Maintaining customizing data programmatically in BC Sets

You might want to automate the lifecycle of your customizing data. For instance, you can update a BC Set with the latest customizing data after some changes. To do this, maintain your changes in EML within the configuration object on a customizing request. Then read the latest data from the underlying customizing tables and finally update the BC Set.

You can programmatically create a customizing request using the XCO CTS module. EML will then be used to maintain the data on that customizing request. Additionally, you have the option to programmatically release the customizing request afterward.

CLASS zcl_errorcode_generate_000 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES tyt_errorcodes            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrcode_000 WITH KEY error_code.
    TYPES tyt_errorcodestext        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zerrcodet_000 WITH KEY langu error_code.
    TYPES tyt_errorcodes_update     TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCode000.
    TYPES tyt_errorcodestext_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCode000Text.

    METHODS delete_errorcodes
      IMPORTING transportrequest TYPE trkorr.

    METHODS create_errorcodes
      IMPORTING transportrequest      TYPE trkorr
                it_errorcodes_crt     TYPE tyt_errorcodes
                it_errorcodestext_crt TYPE tyt_errorcodestext.

    METHODS update_errorcodes
      IMPORTING transportrequest         TYPE trkorr
                it_errorcodes_update     TYPE tyt_errorcodes_update
                it_errorcodestext_update TYPE tyt_errorcodestext_update.

    CONSTANTS co_cust_transport_target      TYPE if_xco_transport_target=>tv_value      VALUE ''.                                          " LOCAL
    CONSTANTS co_cust_transport_description TYPE sxco_ar_short_description              VALUE 'Maintain new error codes programmatically'.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_errorcode_generate_000 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    " Create customizing transport request
    DATA(lo_customizing_request) = xco_cp_cts=>transports->customizing( co_cust_transport_target )->create_request(
        co_cust_transport_description ).

    " Programmatically create new error codes using EML
    create_errorcodes( transportrequest      = lo_customizing_request->value
                       it_errorcodes_crt     = VALUE tyt_errorcodes( ( error_code = '404' )
                                                                     ( error_code = '405' )
                                                                     ( error_code = '406' ) )
                       it_errorcodestext_crt = VALUE tyt_errorcodestext(
                           ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '404' description = 'Not Found' )
                           ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '404' description = 'Nicht gefunden' )
                           ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '405' description = 'Method Not Allowed' )
                           ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '405' description = 'Methode nicht erlaubt' )
                           ( langu  = 'D' error_code  = '406' description = 'Not Acceptable' )
                           ( langu  = 'E' error_code  = '406' description = 'Nicht akzeptabel' ) ) ).

    " Release customizing tasks and customizing request
    DATA(lt_transport_tasks) = lo_customizing_request->get_tasks( ).

    LOOP AT lt_transport_tasks INTO DATA(lo_transport_task).
      IF lo_transport_task->get_status( ) <> xco_cp_transport=>status->modifiable.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

      lo_transport_task->release( ).
    ENDLOOP.

    lo_customizing_request->release( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create_errorcodes.
    DATA lt_errorcode_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZI_ErrorCode000_S\_ErrorCode000.
    TYPES tys_errorcode_create LIKE LINE OF lt_errorcode_create.
    DATA ls_errorcode_create      TYPE tys_errorcode_create.

    DATA lt_errorcodestext_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZI_ErrorCode000\_ErrorCode000Text.
    TYPES tys_errorcodestext_create LIKE LINE OF lt_errorcodestext_create.
    DATA ls_errorcodestext_create TYPE tys_errorcodestext_create.

    LOOP AT it_errorcodes_crt INTO DATA(wa_errorcode_crt).

      ls_errorcode_create = VALUE #( SingletonID = 1
                                     %target     = VALUE #( ( ErrorCode          = wa_errorcode_crt-error_code
                                                              %control-ErrorCode = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).
      APPEND ls_errorcode_create TO lt_errorcode_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_crt INTO DATA(wa_errorcodestext_crt).
      ls_errorcodestext_create = VALUE #(
          %tky-ErrorCode = wa_errorcodestext_crt-error_code
          %target        = VALUE #( ( Langu                = wa_errorcodestext_crt-langu
                                      %control-Langu       = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                      Description          = wa_errorcodestext_crt-description
                                      %control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ).

      APPEND ls_errorcodestext_create TO lt_errorcodestext_create.

    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode000All
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCode000All
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCode000 FROM lt_errorcode_create
           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_crt)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_crt) ##NEEDED.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode000
           CREATE BY \_ErrorCode000Text FROM lt_errorcodestext_create
           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodestext_crt)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodestext_crt)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodestext_crt) ##NEEDED.

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_create)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_create) ##NEEDED.
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_errorcodes.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode000All
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( TransportRequestID )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonId        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           FAILED DATA(f_delete_tr)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete_tr)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete_tr) ##NEEDED.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode000All
           CREATE FIELDS ( TransportRequestID ) WITH VALUE #( ( TransportRequestID  = transportrequest ) )
           DELETE FROM VALUE #( ( SingletonID = 1 ) )
           FAILED DATA(f_delete)
           MAPPED DATA(m_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(r_delete) ##NEEDED.

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_delete)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_delete) ##NEEDED.
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_errorcodes.
    DATA lt_errorcodestext_update_i TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZI_ErrorCode000Text.

    IF it_errorcodes_update IS NOT INITIAL OR it_errorcodestext_update IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT it_errorcodestext_update INTO DATA(ls_errorcodetext_update).
        ls_errorcodetext_update-%control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
        APPEND ls_errorcodetext_update TO lt_errorcodestext_update_i.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           ENTITY ErrorCode000All
           UPDATE
           FIELDS ( transportrequestid )
           WITH VALUE #( ( SingletonID        = 1
                           TransportRequestID = transportrequest ) )

           ENTITY ErrorCode000Text
           UPDATE FROM lt_errorcodestext_update_i

           REPORTED DATA(r_errorcodes_upd)
           FAILED   DATA(f_errorcodes_upd)
           MAPPED   DATA(m_errorcodes_upd) ##NEEDED.

    COMMIT ENTITIES
           BEGIN RESPONSE OF ZI_ErrorCode000_S
           FAILED DATA(failed_update)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_update) ##NEEDED.
    COMMIT ENTITIES END.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

The advantage of using EML to create, update, and delete customizing entries is that all validations of the business configuration object (BCO) are performed. This approach also supports both customizing entries of type table data (TABU) and individual transaction object (TDAT) since API adapters are available for both cases to be used in the behavior implementation of the BCO (for example, mbc_cp_api=>rap_tdat_cts()). For further information, refer to Transport Handling.

After updating the customizing data in the Business Configuration Object (BCO) using EML, the latest customizing data can be retrieved from the underlying customizing tables for further processing. Once the up-to-date customizing data is obtained, it can be directly maintained in the BC Set. This process utilizes the XCO generation APIs for BC Sets.

CLASS zcl_errorcode_generate_000 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CONSTANTS co_workbench_request          TYPE trkorr                                 VALUE ''.                                          " ZLOCAL
    CONSTANTS co_error_codes_set_name       TYPE if_xco_business_cnfgrtn_set=>tv_name   VALUE 'Z_ERROR_CODES_SET_000'.

    CONSTANTS co_error_codes_entity_primary TYPE if_xco_bcs_entity=>tv_name             VALUE 'ZERRCODE_000'.
    CONSTANTS co_error_codes_entity_text    TYPE if_xco_bcs_entity=>tv_name             VALUE 'ZERRCODET_000'.
    CONSTANTS co_error_codes_tobj           TYPE if_xco_bcs_customizing_object=>tv_name VALUE 'ZERRORCODE000'.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_errorcode_generate_000 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    " Read latest error codes from customizing tables
    SELECT * FROM zerrcode_000 WHERE error_code < 500 INTO TABLE (lt_errorcodes).
    ASSERT sy-subrc = 0.

    SELECT * FROM zerrcodet_000 WHERE error_code < 500 INTO TABLE (lt_errorcodes_text).
    ASSERT sy-subrc = 0.

    " Retrieve error codes BC set and make sure it exists
    DATA(lo_business_configuration_set) = xco_cp_abap_repository=>object->scp1->for( co_error_codes_set_name ).
    ASSERT lo_business_configuration_set->if_xco_ar_object~exists( ).

    " Create BC Set patch operation and change specification to remove existing error codes customizing object (all its Entities and corresponding records)
    DATA(lo_patch_operation_delete) = xco_cp_generation=>environment->dev_system( co_workbench_request )->for-scp1->create_patch_operation( ).
    DATA(lo_change_specification_delete) = lo_patch_operation_delete->add_object( co_error_codes_set_name )->create_change_specification( ).
    lo_change_specification_delete->for-delete->add_individual_transaction_obj( co_error_codes_tobj ).

    " Create BC Set patch operation and change specification to insert latest error codes using latest customizing data
    DATA(lo_patch_operation_insert) = xco_cp_generation=>environment->dev_system( co_workbench_request )->for-scp1->create_patch_operation( ).
    DATA(lo_change_specification_insert) = lo_patch_operation_insert->add_object( co_error_codes_set_name )->create_change_specification( ).
    DATA(lo_tobj_insert) = lo_change_specification_insert->for-insert->add_individual_transaction_obj(
                               co_error_codes_tobj ).
    lo_tobj_insert->for-insert->add_entity( co_error_codes_entity_primary )->for-insert->add_records(
                                                                              REF #( lt_errorcodes[] ) ).
    lo_tobj_insert->for-insert->add_entity( co_error_codes_entity_text )->for-insert->add_records(
                                                                           REF #( lt_errorcodes_text[] ) ).

    " After preparing the change specifications, execute the PATCH operations
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_result_delete) = lo_patch_operation_delete->execute( ) ##NEEDED.
        DATA(lo_result_insert) = lo_patch_operation_insert->execute( ) ##NEEDED.

      CATCH cx_xco_gen_patch_exception INTO DATA(lo_xco_gen_patch_exception).

        LOOP AT lo_xco_gen_patch_exception->findings->get( ) ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_finding>) ##NEEDED.
          " Process errors/exceptions raised from the PATCH operation
        ENDLOOP.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

This section of using the XCO generation API for BC Sets to update BC set data requires customizing entries of type individual transaction object (TDAT). This is because it’s a requirement for importing any data into a BC Set. BC Sets are workbench objects and can be delivered alongside other development objects in your development software components.

Hint: In productive scenarios remove the ##NEEDED pragma from the code examples and implement proper error handling!

Summary

This blog post explains how a SaaS provider can deliver customizing data, maintained using a custom business configuration maintenance object, by utilizing BC Sets. It covers how to import customizing data into BC Sets, how to unpack such data by using an application job. Finally, we have a look into how to programmatically maintain customizing data in BC Sets.

entry.title: Re: India Process Ewaybill for PO Return Delivery Questio...
entry.content:
Part 1 — Initial Pickup under EXW

  • You (buyer) are responsible for movement since EXW terms apply.
  • Value > ₹50,000, so you correctly generate the e-Way Bill on the GST portal before pickup.
  • Reference document: Purchase Order / Invoice from vendor.

Part 2 — Return of 3 Damaged Items

  • GST Compliance Requirement: When goods are returned, movement must be supported by a valid document (return challan or debit note).
  • Outside SAP: Yes, you can create a manual return delivery challan outside SAP and use it to generate the e-Way Bill. This is compliant as long as:
    • The challan clearly states “Return of goods” with reference to the original invoice/PO.
    • The e-Way Bill is generated against this challan.

Risk: If you bypass SAP, you lose system traceability. From a compliance perspective, GST law allows manual challans, but from an audit perspective, it’s better to have SAP documents aligned.

Part 3 — Replacement of 3 Damaged Items

  • Separate e-Way Bill is required because this is a fresh movement of goods from vendor to buyer.
  • Reference Document Options:
    1. Vendor’s Replacement Invoice (if vendor issues a new invoice for the replacement items).
    2. Vendor’s Delivery Challan (if replacement is done without a commercial invoice, e.g., free replacement under warranty).
    3. In this case, the challan must clearly mention “Replacement against return challan no. …”.
  • As buyer, you generate the e-Way Bill using the vendor’s replacement document as reference.


Best Practice Recommendation

  • Return: Use a return challan (SAP or manual) → generate e-Way Bill.
  • Replacement: Use vendor’s replacement invoice or delivery challan → generate e-Way Bill.
  • Even though manual documents are legally valid, it is strongly recommended to align with SAP processes for audit trail and reconciliation. Otherwise, finance and compliance teams will struggle to match returns and replacements.

entry.title: Re: How to get details of serial number or equipment master for GI Deliveries?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

" 

Finally, as result you will get:

  • Material (OBJK-MATNR)
  • Equipment (OBJK-EQUNR)
  • Serial Number (OBJK-SERNR)
  • Notification (OBJK-IHNUM)

You can have SAP Query by using following Table to get the desired result.

  • LIKP - SD Document: Delivery Header Data
  • SER01 - Document Header for Serial Numbers for Delivery
  • OBJK - Plant Maintenance Object List
  • LIPS - SD document: Delivery: Item data    

" 

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: QM: Usage Decision allowed without Results Recording – How to restrict
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP QM, For an Inspection Lot I want to restrict its Usage Decision  until Results Recording is completed.

The inspection characteristics are already maintained as Required Characteristics, but even if I skip Results Recording, the system still allows me to complete the Usage Decision.

Currently, I only get a warning message ( for example: Inspection completion forced ), but UD is not blocked.

Is there a standard SAP way to convert this into a hard restriction, so that UD is not possible unless Results Recording is done?

Or is a user exit / BAdI required for this scenario?

Any inputs or standard SAP references would be helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How to Implement KeyFigure based Validation Rules in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Templates
entry.content:
Lot of times users have requirements when they want to restrict the value of a certain measure to allow users to only input certain values. Eg. You have a quantity measure and you want to restrict users from entering a negative value.

Similarly you may have to run some validation rules on the measures that user is inputting in the template to make sure the calculations are correct. Lets take an example.

We have Budget amount that user inputs in the template. Then you have monthly spread where he needs to input how much budget they will spend in each of the months in the budget year.

Then another amount is input by the user which is after budget year so this is the budget they will spend after the budget year and finally you have the unutilized budget which is Budget amount – monthly spread – After budget year. So in this case the unutilized budget should not be negative.

Such kind of checks are required in the input templates. We have option to create validation rules at the model level but these rules check  if planners can only enter data successfully for the valid dimension member combinations specified in the validation rule. 

So these validation rules cant be used in such requirements. The only option in our case if to use SAC scripts.

Below is the script that can be written in the OnResultChanged Event of the table.

var array = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet() ;

for (var i=0;i<array.length;i++)

{

 InputKey =  ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].id ) ;

         if (InputKey  === "Key" )

         {                       InputKey _val = ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].formattedValue ) ;

                           if (InputKey _val === "1")

                                           {}

                             else if(InputKey _val === "2")

                                  {}

                             else if(InputKey_val === "3")

                                            {}

                             else if (InputKey _val === "4")

                                            {}

                             else if (InputKey _val === "5")

                                            {}

                  else

                                           {

Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Key must be between 1 to 5");

                                             }

        }

Quantity =  ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].id ) ;   

  if (Quantity  === "Quantity" )

              {    Quantity_Keyval = ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].formattedValue ) ;

                            if (Quantity_Keyval< "0")

                                           {

                         Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Quantity cannot be negative");

              }

       }

 BudgetUn =  ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].id ) ;

 if (BudgetUn  === "30021035-8936-4284-3557-115183456351" )

// Check if unutilized Budget is negative

              {

                             BudgetUn_Keyval = ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].formattedValue ) ;

                             if (BudgetUn_Keyval< "0")

                                           {

                                                         Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Unutilized Budget cannot be negative");

                        }

}            

}

Also make sure below list of variables are created in the Outline. All the variables will be of type string.

Quantity

Quantity_Keyval

BudgetUn

BudgetUn_Keyval

InputKey

InputKey_val

If you want to use any calculated or a restricted measure in the validation you need to check the technical name of the measure in console. Eg. See below console screenshot which shows the technical name of the Unutilized Budget amount.

Once you implement the script and execute the story, if user enters any invalid values then we get an error message as below:

However after closing the error message if I still try to publish the data the system will publish and will not check anything.

So now we have two options either we ask the planners to be responsible enough and not to publish anything if they get error messages.

The other option is to implement a custom publish button and disable standard publish in the story.

Below script can be added in the custom publish button so that if users enter any invalid values they cannot publish the data.

var array = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet() ;

var temp = 0 ;

for (var i=0;i<array.length;i++)

{            Quantity =  ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].id ) ;

               if (Quantity  === "Quantity" )

              {

                             Quantity_Keyval = ( array[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].formattedValue ) ;

                             if (Quantity_Keyval >=0 )

                                           {}

                  else

                                           { temp = 1 ;

                                           }

                  }

 }

if (temp === 1)

              {

Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Quantity cannot be negative");

              }

else

            {

                Table_1.getPlanning().getPublicVersion("Budget").publish();

                 }

Thus as shown above the key figure based validation option provides a powerful alternative in SAC planning templates to ensure users enter valid set of values before publishing the data.

entry.title: Re: Edocument cockpit for ksef
entry.content:
Good day @jerrysam 

Thank you for your question.

Based on the screenshot you provided, your tax code exists, but the KSeF tax mapping seems to be incomplete or inconsistent for one or more tax codes your invoice uses.

I suggest you check your eDocument Cockpit, open one document that failed.

Check the Log and note the tax code mentioned. Confirm that same tax codes are used in the billing document line items.

Make sure your correct or maintain the configuration for Mapping Tax Codes to Invoice Elements for every affected tax code (Procedure attributes required for KSeF mappting, Account key linkage, etc). If one tax code is missing or not fully maintained, you will get that submission error.

 See the following references for more details:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3683067

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb525a45b55f4a1c9bb32c254affeeec/8c5785f19e144c67be62de3567073c52.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Customer invioce VAT 0,01 differnce coming
entry.content:
• SAP calculates VAT line by line (per item/GL line) and then sums the tax amounts.
  • Many external systems (or manual books) calculate VAT on the total net amount and then apply the tax percentage once.
  • Because of rounding rules (usually to 2 decimals), the two approaches can differ by 0.01 or more.

In your example:

  • Net = 107.23 (sum of credits excluding VAT).
  • 22% VAT = 23.59 (books).
  • SAP line-by-line calculation = 23.60.
  • Difference = 0.01.

So the practical options are

  • Option A (Preferred): Adjust your upload program to let SAP calculate VAT.
  • Option B: Change tax procedure rounding to “calculate on total” if that matches statutory requirements.
  • Option C: Use tolerance settings to absorb 0.01 differences automatically.

entry.title: Re: India Process Ewaybill for PO Return Delivery Questio...
entry.content: The “challan” in Part 3 is the vendor’s delivery challan (not yours), and it should explicitly mention that the goods are being supplied as a replacement against your earlier return challan. That way, the e-Way Bill has a clear audit trail linking the return and the replacement.

entry.title: Re: SAP POs to Ariba B&I without Commerce Automation
entry.content:
Hi @velayudham 
This is the standard when you have B&I.
Here you can have the data flow of the CIG integration: https://help.sap.com/docs/sisgw/sap-ariba-cloud-integration-gateway-overview-guide/sap-ariba-buying-and-invoicing?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

By default:

  1. Requisitionner creates a PR and submit it for approval,  after submition, PR can be created in S4 with the Real Time budget Check (optional).
  2. When the PR is approved in B&I, another budget check happens to make sure the budget is still avalable
  3. Then a PO is created in Ariba B&I, the the PO is sent to S4 t, and the S4 PO number is send back to Ariba B&I 
  4. Afterward, the Carbon Copy of the PO is sent directly to Supplier on SAP SBN, 
  5. Supplier can send Order Confimation to Ariba B&I (not sent to S4)
  6. Supplier can send Ship Notice to B&I (not sent to S4)
  7. Upon the goods reception, the requisitionner creates a Goods Receipt which is then sent and posted in S4
  8. The Supplier on SBN send the Invoice to B&I
  9. Invoice Reconcisliation happens in B&I and 
  10. Then invoice is posted in S4
  11. Buyer pays the Supplier to finalise the process.

entry.title: ABAP AI Podcast: Reflecting on 2025 and Looking Ahead to 2026
entry.content:
2025: The Year ABAP AI Became Tangible

As Simona and I reflected on 2025, one theme stood out: it was the year ABAP AI stopped being a promise and became something developers could touch, use, and trust. That shift didn’t happen all at once — it was shaped across the year through a series of releases that progressively deepened what ABAP and AI could do together.

The journey began with Release 2502, our first big delivery, which marked a turning point for the ABAP developer experience. This release introduced the foundational AI capabilities that developers immediately felt in their workflow: the initial wave of explain ability for ABAP and CDS, predictive and context‑aware code suggestions, and early ABAP Cloud compliance insights. 2502 was a statement: ABAP AI had arrived, and it was ready for real use cases.

Building on this momentum, Release 2505 expanded and strengthened the story. the RAP BO Generator for Fiori applications. The explain model grew to cover more ABAP constructs, predictive assistance became more capable, and clean‑core compliance checks were enhanced to give developers clearer, more actionable guidance.

Toward the end of the year, Release 2508 brought refinement and specialization. AI guidance became more scenario‑aware — better tuned for migration, modernization, and, AI‑supported extensibility patterns. Autogenerated documentation became richer and more reliable. Custom code migration also received a boost with deeper AI‑powered insights and more intelligent documentation support — small but meaningful improvements that helped teams move faster and with more confidence.

And throughout all of this, we made sure customers in private cloud were not left behind. With the 2025 PCE add‑ins, Joule for Developers became available to S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition customers as well — enabling AI‑assisted custom code migration, modernization support, and clean‑core guidance even for those on older releases. This was a major step in democratizing ABAP AI and bringing its value to a much broader installed base.

Key Features and Developer Impact

With Joule for Developers, ABAP developers immediately benefited from a range of intelligent capabilities, including:

  • Predictive and context‑aware code suggestions
  • Automated ABAP Cloud compliance checks
  • Natural‑language explanations for both legacy and new ABAP code and extended explain use for autogenerated technical documentation
  • Developers could even generate complete Fiori applications simply by describing the functional requirements.

These advancements significantly accelerated development cycles and reduced manual effort. AI also enhanced custom code migration by proposing simplifications, improving code quality, and easing the transition to clean‑core architectures.

ABAP and AI: A Strengthened Relationship

Simona shared insights into why ABAP required SAP‑specific AI models. Because ABAP is proprietary, large language models were not initially trained on it. SAP addressed this by training internal models on over 250 million lines of ABAP code and 30 million lines of CDS views, unlocking high‑quality AI support tailored specifically to ABAP. 

To hear the full conversation, listen to the episode: ABAP AI Podcast: Reflecting on 2025 and Looking Ahead to 2026

Another important point we touched on in the conversation is how ABAP AI brings together the best of both worlds. SAP pairs its own ABAP‑specialized models—trained on hundreds of millions of lines of ABAP and CDS code—with best‑of‑breed third‑party foundation models. This hybrid approach ensures developers always get the most accurate and context‑aware support for ABAP tasks, while still benefiting from the broad language understanding and creativity of large general‑purpose models. And for customers who are primarily interested in explain ability, SAP also offers the explain‑only model through the AI Hub, making ABAP‑focused code explanations accessible even without the full Joule for Developers experience.

Far from replacing developers, AI strengthens the ABAP ecosystem. The ABAP community has shown excitement and readiness to embrace these advancements, seeing AI as a powerful augmentation of their skills.                                  

2026: Scaling the Promise

Looking forward, 2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year of scaling ABAP AI. SAP plans to bring AI capabilities to customers running older private cloud releases through a side‑by‑side version of Joule for Developer, ensuring broader access also for customers on older S4HANA versions.  

SAP will also add to it agentic tools and frameworks and an ABAP MCP Server, enabling SAP teams and partners to build intelligent agents that automate development tasks and connect seamlessly into an integrated agentic ecosystem. Investment in AI‑assisted custom code migration will continue, focusing on clean‑core compliance and automated code simplification proposals.

VS Code and Modern Tooling

A major priority for 2026 is establishing VS Code as a first‑class development environment for ABAP. By combining VS Code’s modern developer experience with Joule and AI‑centric capabilities, SAP aims to reposition ABAP as a future‑proof, cloud‑native, enterprise‑grade language.

Trust and Enterprise Readiness

As AI evolves, SAP continues to emphasize safety, compliance, and enterprise‑grade ethics. All AI use cases undergo rigorous validation to meet SAP’s security and trust standards.

Custom Code Migration (CCM)

A core focus area we highlighted in the podcast is the continued evolution of Custom Code Migration (CCM) and its central role in helping customers transition to clean‑core, cloud‑ready architectures. Throughout 2025, AI became a powerful companion in this journey — analyzing legacy patterns, identifying simplification opportunities, and guiding developers toward more future‑proof designs. With Joule for Developers, teams benefit from usage‑aware insights, automated refactoring suggestions, and richer technical documentation that dramatically reduces the manual effort usually tied to migration projects. As we move into 2026, CCM is set to scale even further, with deeper automation and more intelligent guidance that make modernization and S/4HANA transitions faster, more predictable, and far less error‑prone.

Conclusion

The year 2025 proved the real, tangible value of ABAP AI. In 2026, ABAP and AI will become inseparable—empowering developers with intelligent tools that make coding faster, smarter, and more reliable. The vision is clear: the future of ABAP development is deeply AI‑enhanced, enabling organizations to innovate with confidence and speed.

entry.title: Re: Workaround to confirm a Combined Order operation with...
entry.content:
Hi @ann_hustis I suggested to use BAPIs (SAP provided interfaces) not BAdIs (enhancement points). In my opinion there is no way to rework CO19 to post a time ticket.

What can be done is to implement a custom transaction that would get the same data as CO19. It would store a "start" event in a custom DB table. Then when a "end" event is entered in the transaction, it could post a time ticket with the BAPI. So users could use the custom transaction in pretty much the same way as CO19, but finally the confirmation would be posted as a time ticket.

entry.title: Exploring Enhanced Mass Actions and Compensation Forms in Job Scheduler
entry.content:
In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the enhanced capabilities of the Job Scheduler feature, with a special focus on Mass Actions and Compensation Forms for templates. I’ll also share a few tested scenarios to demonstrate how these enhancements work in real-world use cases, making job scheduling more efficient and flexible than ever.

About Mass Action:

Mass Action in Executive Review now supports additional tabs, fields, and enhanced functionality. With this update, Mass Action can be applied across the Salary, Bonus, and Stock tabs. In addition to numeric values, you can now apply Mass Actions using percentage values, providing greater flexibility when managing compensation changes.

Field Eligibility and Considerations
The following conditions and field types determine whether a field can be updated using Mass Action:
• Standard fields must be editable, included in the template, and users must have field-based write permissions.
• Supported standard field IDs include:
• Salary tab (previously supported): merit, promo, extra, extra2, lumpsum, lumpsum2
• Bonus tab: bonusAdjustment
• Stock tab: stock, stockUnits, options, stockOther1, stockOther2, stockOther3
• Custom fields that are editable, reportable, and have appropriate field-based write permissions are supported.
• Fields with dropdown (enum) selections are supported.
• Money and amount custom fields that support totals are supported.
• For percent-type fields, only percentage-based changes are allowed.
• Money and Amount fields can be updated either by a percentage or a fixed amount.
• String fields display a text input when using Mass Action.
• Dropdown fields display available values for selection during Mass Action.
• By default, only fields with existing values are updated by Mass Action. However, String and enum fields are updated even if their values are blank or null.

These enhancements significantly expand the applicability of Mass Action, enabling support for a wider range of fields and business scenarios.

How It looks Now:

In the current version, the Mass Action functionality is available for additional standard fields on the stock and bonus compensation worksheet tabs and can also be applied to custom fields on any tab meeting certain criteria. Also, you can apply modifications to the entered values in percentage terms when relevant.

 

How It looks Before:

In the previous version, Mass Action was only available for select standard fields on the Salary tab. Also, you could not make the modifications to the Program in percent values for relevant fields.

 

 

Granting Permissions for the Mass Action Feature

Before using the Mass Action feature, ensure that the appropriate permissions are granted to users.
Navigation Path:
Manage Permission Roles → Compensation and Variable Pay → Mass Action – Compensation

 

Testing Mass Action Scenarios in Executive Review

The validation of the Mass Action feature in Executive Review by testing cost increase scenarios across different programs. Each scenario documents the end-to-end process used to apply the change and verify the results through reporting.


Scenario 1: Increasing the “Options” Program Cost by 2%

In this scenario, the Options program cost was increased by 2% using the Mass Action functionality. The following steps were performed to execute and validate the update:

  1. Navigate to Executive Review.
  2. Select the current planning template.
  3. Click the Mass Action button from the worksheet toolbar.
  4. The Mass Action pop-up window opens.
  5. From the Programs drop-down menu, select Options.
  6. From the Action drop-down menu, select Increase.
  7. In the Modify the total program cost by section:
  • Enter the percentage increase (for example, 2).
  • Select the % checkbox.
  • Click Apply.

8. A confirmation pop-up appears. Click OK to proceed.

9. Once the job is successfully submitted, click OK again to close the confirmation message.

10. From the module picker drop-down, navigate to Reporting and select View Schedules.

11. Verify that the job status is marked as Completed.

12. Under the Action section, download the report.

13. Review the report to validate the Before and After cost changes for the Options program.

Please refer to the screenshots below for visual guidance and confirmation of the steps.

 

 

Scenario 2: Increasing the “Stock (RSUs)” Program Cost by 5%

This scenario validates a 5% increase to the Stock (RSUs) program using the same Mass Action workflow. The steps followed are outlined below:

  1. Navigate to Executive Review.
  2. Select the current planning template.
  3. Click the Mass Action button from the worksheet toolbar.
  4. The Mass Action pop-up window opens.
  5. From the Programs drop-down menu, select RSUs.
  6. From the Action drop-down menu, select Increase.
  7. In the Modify the total program cost by section:
  • Enter the percentage increase (for example, 5).
  • Select the % checkbox.
  • Click Apply.

8. A confirmation pop-up appears. Click OK to proceed.

9. Once the job is successfully submitted, click OK again to close the confirmation message.

10. From the module picker drop-down, navigate to Reporting and select View Schedules.

11. Confirm that the job status is Completed.

12. Under the Action section, download the report.

13. Review the report to validate the Before and After cost changes for the RSUs program.

Screenshots are provided below to support and visually confirm the execution and validation steps.

 

 

 

Update Compensation Forms for Template – Scheduled Job


What Is It?
The Update Compensation Forms for Template job can now be scheduled and executed directly from the Scheduled Job Manager in Admin Tools. This enhancement enables administrators to run the job on demand or schedule it for later, without requiring support intervention.


Why Is It Important?
With this update, you can configure and monitor the job using the Job Scheduler and Job Monitor for compensation worksheets. The job uses the same parameters as the Update All Worksheets tool, ensuring consistent results while providing greater flexibility and control.

Configuration Requirement
As this is a customer-configured upgrade, to enable the feature:
1. Navigate to Scheduled Job Manager → Job Scheduler → Create Job Request
2. Enable the option to create a job of type Update Compensation Forms for Template

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites
To configure and run this job, the following permissions are required:
• Administrator Permissions → Admin Center Permissions → Manage Scheduled Jobs
• Administrator Permissions → Admin Center Permissions → Monitor Scheduled Jobs
• Administrator Permissions → Manage Compensation → Update Compensation Forms for Template.


Scheduling the “Update Compensation Forms” Job from the Frontend

After the H225 release, I had the opportunity to schedule the Update Compensation Forms job directly from the frontend, rather than through provisioning. This approach worked as expected and successfully executed the job based on the configured parameters.

Below is the step-by-step process followed to create, schedule, and manage the job

  1. Navigate to Scheduled Job Manager.
  2. Click the Job Scheduler tab.
  3. Click Create Job Request.
  4. Enter the following details:
  5. Job Name – Enter a name for the job.
  6. Job Type – Select Update Compensation Forms for Template.
  7. Job Owner – Select the user recorded as running the job. The owner must have all required permissions. Example: Emily Clark ‎(admin)
  8. Compensation Template – Select the current Compensation planning template.
  9. Job Parameters – Job parameters vary by job type. Select the following checkboxes as required:

I. Add newly eligible employees to the compensation worksheet.

II. Update the worksheet to reflect employee eligibility changes.

ii(a). Reapply defined eligibility rules before updating the worksheet.

10.  Job Occurrence – Define how frequently the job should run.

11. Time Zone – Select a time zone from the dropdown. By default, the time zone is UTC+0000 (UTC).

If Dependent Of is selected for the Occurrence field, the Time Zone field is disabled and inherited from the prerequisite job.

12. Occurrence (Recurring) – Select a recurrence pattern: Daily, Weekly, Monthly, or Yearly.

13. Select the Start and End date and time.

14. Notification Recipients – Enter additional email recipients (comma-separated) to receive job notifications, excluding the job owner.

Send Email When Job Fails – Select this option to send an email notification if the job fails.

Send Email When Job Completes – Select this option to send an email notification when the job completes successfully.

15. Click Submit to save and schedule the job to run at the specified time. OR

16. Click Save to save the job request without scheduling it.

Note: This job can be stopped in two ways:

  1. Specify an End Date during job scheduling. The job will automatically stop on the specified date.
  2. To stop the job manually, follow the steps below:
  3. Click the Job Scheduler tab.
  4. In the Job Type section, select the job type (for example, Update Compensation Forms for Template) and click Go.
  5. Select the appropriate job from the list, click Actions, and then click Cancel.

 

 

Note: All screenshots shown are from the Salesdemo instance.
